---
name: self-improvement-loop
description: "Use when correcting recurring mistakes, stale instructions, or workflow drift so future tasks improve."
---

# Self Improvement Loop

## When to Use

Use this skill when you detect recurring errors, conflicting instructions, stale workflow docs, or repeated avoidable rework.

## When Not to Use

Do not use this skill for first-time issues that have no evidence of repetition.

## Files and Surfaces

- .github/skills/SKILL_MAP.md
- .github/skills/
- .github/prompts/
- docs/
- /memories/

## Deliverables

- a brief root cause statement
- the smallest instruction update that prevents recurrence
- verification that the fix removed the mismatch or ambiguity
- a short lesson captured in memory if broadly reusable

## Improvement Rules

- Fix process causes, not only symptoms.
- Prefer precise instruction edits over broad policy additions.
- Remove or tighten stale guidance instead of stacking duplicates.
- Update the skill map whenever skill behavior changes.

## Verification Checklist

- Root cause is identified and documented.
- Updated guidance is specific and testable.
- Affected files are aligned with the new guidance.
- Memory is updated only when the insight is reusable.
